evals.name: fresh-language
evals.prompt: I have a Nuxt Content site with English markdown pages in `content/en/`. Please translate them all to French so I get a `content/fr/` folder.
evals.expected_output: A new `content/fr/` folder is created, mirroring the source structure: `content/fr/index.md` and `content/fr/blog/post-1.md`. Frontmatter `title` and `description` values are translated to French while the keys and structural fields (`navigation: true`, `date: 2026-01-15`, `draft: false`) are preserved. Markdown body prose, headings, and list items are translated. The link `[installation guide](/docs/install)` keeps its `/docs/install` URL with only the link text translated. A `content/.metadata/` folder is created with `translated.json` (`["blog/post-1.md", "index.md"]`), `translated-langs.json` (`["fr"]`), and `hashes.json` (one SHA-1 per source file path).

evals.name: markdown-preservation
evals.prompt: Translate my Nuxt Content pages to French.
evals.expected_output: `content/fr/getting-started.md` is created. Translated: frontmatter `title` and `description`, the headings and prose, and the `[API reference](/docs/api)` link text. Preserved byte-for-byte: the fenced ```ts code block (imports, `createClient`, `process.env.API_KEY`), inline code `createClient`, the `/docs/api` URL, the frontmatter keys and structural values (`draft: false`, `layout: docs`), and the MDC component `::callout{icon="i-heroicons-light-bulb" color="amber"}` â€” its name, `icon`, and `color` props stay identical while the callout body text is translated. The closing `::` is kept.

evals.name: incremental
evals.prompt: I changed the user guide and added a changelog page to my English content. Can you sync the French and German translations? The Nuxt config has the languages.
evals.expected_output: Only the changed page (`guide.md`, whose stored hash no longer matches) and the new page (`changelog.md`, not yet in `translated.json`) are queued â€” `index.md` is skipped because its hash still matches. New files `content/fr/changelog.md` and `content/de/changelog.md` are created, and `content/fr/guide.md` / `content/de/guide.md` are re-translated (now mentioning CSV/PDF export). `content/fr/index.md` and `content/de/index.md` are left untouched. `.metadata/translated.json` becomes `["changelog.md", "guide.md", "index.md"]`, `translated-langs.json` stays `["de", "fr"]`, and `hashes.json` is updated so `guide.md` and `changelog.md` hold the current source SHA-1.
